role_adherence_notes.linguistic: Correctly identified lack of visible text; stayed within domain.
role_adherence_notes.landscape: Analyzed terrain and climate cues consistent with Andean highlands.
role_adherence_notes.botanics: Analyzed vegetation types (grasses/scrub) appropriate for the domain.
role_adherence_notes.regulatory: Analyzed infrastructure and road features; correctly noted absence of signs.
role_adherence_notes.meta: Synthesized construction styles and utility infrastructure.

visual_consistency_notes.landscape: Descriptions of terrain and grass match image.
visual_consistency_notes.regulatory: Descriptions of road and curb match image.
visual_consistency_notes.meta: Descriptions of construction and drains match image.

verdict.overall_verdict_notes: The agents provided high-quality, grounded assessments of the visual evidence but failed to distinguish between Colombia and Ecuador due to the shared Andean characteristics. The judge correctly synthesized the evidence to select the most probable candidate (Colombia) from the available options, despite the ground truth being Ecuador.
verdict.region_narrowing_notes: The judge correctly identified the Andean region (South America) based on the unanimous agreement of all agents regarding the terrain, vegetation, and construction style.
verdict.hypothesis_pool_notes: The pool included Colombia and Ecuador, which are the two most plausible candidates given the visual evidence. However, the pool was limited to these two, missing other Andean possibilities like Peru or Bolivia, though the visual evidence strongly favored the northern Andes.
